name: node-unique-filename
title: node-unique-filename
summary: unique filename for use in temporary directories or caches
description:
unique filename for use in temporary directories or caches
This module can be used to generate a unique filename for use in temporary
directories or caches.
.
For example, if you pass os.tmpdir() as an argument, it returns something
like: /tmp/912ec803b2ce49e4a541068d495ab570.
.
It also include small package node-unique-slug that
generate a unique character string suitible for use
in files and URLs.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-unique-filename because unique filename for use in temporary directories or caches.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-unique-filename as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-unique-filename with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-unique-filename --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
